metadata.title:
TO CHANGE


LAWS On


CONTEMPT

Storm of Protest Against
Sentencing Seattle Star


Editors.

pages.raw_text:
(6y United Press Leased Wtre)


SEATTLE, Dec. 19.-A storm Of
protests from the city and from
outside have piled into the Seattle
Star office following the sentenc-
ing Of two editors Of the Seattle
Star late Friday afternoon for con-
tempt of court. The Fremont Bap-
tist church congregation yesterday
unanimously passed a 1000 word
resolution commencing the editors
and pledging them their utmost
support in their fight for the peo.
Pile of the Duwamish valley, now
at the mercy Of the traction trust.


From all over the country wires
have poured into the Star offices
from editors and publicists, urging
them to continue the fight for a
free press and the right to COM
ment on court decisions.


Already bills are being drafted.
taking from judge the right to try
his own contempt cases, and others
wiping the principle of construc.
tive contempt-that is, contempt
outside the court room-from the
statute books of the state.


That the matter will be fought
out in the legislature which meets
early in January seems a certain
ty. The contempt law, involving
the right of free press and the
Injunction law will both come be-
fore the legislature.
